steps.title: Find the sum of the 9 given integers
steps.content: The nine given integers sum to: 43 + 45 + 44 + 43 + 38 + 39 + 40 + 46 + 40 = 378.

steps.title: Set up the equation for the 10th integer
steps.content:
Let $x$ be the unknown 10th integer. If the mean of all 10 integers is $M$ (an integer greater than 42), then:
$$\frac{378 + x}{10} = M$$
Rearranging: $$x = 10M - 378$$

steps.title: Determine M using the constraint that all integers are between 0 and 60
steps.content:
Since $0 < x < 60$:
  • $x > 0 \Rightarrow 10M - 378 > 0 \Rightarrow M > 37.8$
  • $x < 60 \Rightarrow 10M - 378 < 60 \Rightarrow M < 43.8$
With the additional requirement that $M > 42$ and $M$ is an integer, the only possibility is $M = 43$.

steps.title: Solve for x and identify the maximum value
steps.content:
Substituting $M = 43$: $$x = 10(43) - 378 = 52$$
The nine given integers have a maximum of 46. Since 52 > 46, the largest integer in data set A is 52.
